where are the upper motor neurons that control skeletal muscles found The postcentral gyrus, located in the parietal lobe of the brain, is the region where the upper motor neurons that control skeletal muscles are found. These neurons originate from this area and project to the spinal cord to facilitate voluntary muscle movements.
